steps.title: Spot the contrast clue
steps.content: The passage says blue holes and cenotes differ in size, water composition, and type of location, so they are "not usually viewed as being ______" — but then adds that both are water-filled sinkholes formed the same way. The blank names how they are NOT viewed despite those differences. Since they share an origin, the missing word must mean similar or alike.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The word needs to mean similar or alike. Substitute each choice:
  • "distinct": means clearly different — the passage already says they are different; saying they are "not viewed as distinct" would contradict the passage. ✗
  • "explicable": means able to be explained — the passage is not questioning whether they can be explained; this is irrelevant to the contrast. ✗
  • "analogous": means similar or comparable — because they differ on the surface, they are not viewed as analogous (similar), even though their origins match. ✓
  • "incongruous": means mismatched or incompatible — saying they are "not viewed as incongruous" would mean people think they fit together, which doesn’t match the contrast the passage sets up. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
